prevalence.trend: Stable to slightly increasing due to environmental factors

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease (GBD) Study, IHME

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Based on GINA guidelines: Symptoms of wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness; confirmed by spirometry showing reversible airflow obstruction (FEV1/FVC <0.7, improvement >12% post-bronchodilator)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Limited routine screening; opportunistic via primary care (~20-30% awareness in at-risk groups)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Harmonized under EU guidelines, but varies by country (e.g., spirometry access better in Western EU)
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Follows GINA/EPRS: Stepwise inhaled corticosteroids + long-acting beta-agonists; biologics for severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Disparities in access (Eastern vs. Western EU); high costs for biologics; environmental pollution in urban areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Advanced research (e.g., ERS initiatives); widespread primary care integration; EU-wide surveillance via ECDC
